doc: Pitch-drop drum body: one gate-fired envelope both drops the pitch of a sine body (base + decaying sweep) and shapes its amplitude through the nested shaped-vca -- the classic kick/tom 'thump'. At rest it is the kick (base 48 Hz, sweep 220 Hz, tight decay); a tom is the same body with `base`/`sweep`/`release` overridden at the reference (e.g. 130/120/0.12). Every former baked literal is a defaulted interface pipe (canonical names per ADR-0058): `base`/`sweep` set the pitch drop, `attack`/`decay`/`sustain`/`release` reshape the contour (`decay` stretches tail AND pitch drop together -- one envelope drives both). Hosted by a voicer only `gate` is drivable (per-voice params aren't host-addressable); statically nested via `subpatch`, every pipe is reachable on the boundary face. interface: gate in (+ pitch/contour pipes), audio/active out.

nodes.doc: Materializes the `sweep` pipe (pitch-sweep amount, Hz) into a Signal.
Sweep amount (a) * the raw linear envelope CV (b, tapped from the nested shaped-vca) -> the decaying pitch drop.
Materializes the `base` pipe (body base frequency, Hz) into a Signal.
Sine body, frequency driven by the pitch-drop.
The nested shaped-vca (one envelope shapes the amp; its raw `env` CV also drives the pitch drop above). Contour pipes forward this body's own pipes.
